result.title: Insider Tips for Prompting with AI
result.description:
Want to build your own app just by describing it? Vibe coding lets you generate functional software using natural language, though the precision of your prompt dictates the quality of your output.
In this tutorial, we walk through building a fully playable 3D racing game using these core prompting strategies:

- Be Granular and Specific: Open-ended prompts produce generic results. Break your request into structured bullet points or themes to define your vision.
- Dictate Technical Keywords: Guide the model by choosing the underlying technology. Even if you aren't technical, tell the model to find the best language or tools for your specific use case.
Include Visual References: Upload sketches, screenshots, or reference art alongside your text to establish the visual style and layout.
- Iterate and Refine: Treat the initial generation as a baseline. Playtest your application, identify improvements, and issue direct follow-up prompts to perfect the build.

Learn more: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/aistudio-build-mode   
Build your ideas with Gemini: aistudio.google.com/apps   
Ship your site or builds here: http://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/aistudio-build-mode#deploy-or
#VibeCoding #BuildWithGemini #AIStudio

result.title: 100 years of Route 66 #GoogleDoodle
result.description:
Ready for a road trip? 🚗💨🛣️⛽☕ Route 66 turns 100 this year, and today’s #GoogleDoodle pays tribute to the legendary Mother Road with some of the historic letterforms and road signs that make the route memorable. How many of these icons do you recognize? 

G — Delgadillo's Snow Cap Drive-In, California

O — Blue Swallow Motel, New Mexico

O — Historic Route 66 Motel sign, Arizona 

G — Munger Moss Motel, Missouri

L — Munger Moss Motel, Missouri

E — Blue Swallow Motel, New Mexico 

Check out Route 66 on Google Maps to help plan your trip.

📸 Letterforms captured by Pics On Route 66 photographer David J. Schwartz.

result.title: What’s New in Google Accessibility | Episode 12 | American Sign Language
result.description:
Welcome back to another episode of What's New in Google Accessibility, a series where we share the latest product and feature updates that help make Google more accessible for everyone. In this episode, we'll tell you about updates on Android, Pixel, YouTube, Chrome Browser, and Gemini. 

Check out our full series at https://g.co/a11y/playlist 
To watch this video without American Sign Language (ASL) interpretation, please click here: https://youtu.be/s0M79zWRKps
To learn more, sign up for our accessibility newsletter at: https://g.co/a11y/news 

0:00 Introduction
0:25 Left and Right Ear Controls | Android  
1:01 Talkback Keyboard Shortcut | Android 
1:24 3-Button Navigation | Android 
1:55 Mouse Keys Feature | Android 
2:08 Voice Access | Android 
3:22 Guided Frame | Pixel 
4:31 Caret Browsing | Chrome 
5:20 Voice Boost | YouTube
5:45 Tips for the neurodivergent community| Gemini
7:11 Closing
